channel.title: WordPress content updates not reflecting on the front end - Siteground back end
channel.description:
Hi guys, Is Frontity or Vercel caching the WordPress content somehow? I have made changes in the CMS but they are not reflecting on the front end. I have created the file `vercel.json` { "version": 2, "builds": [ { "src": "package.json", "use": "@frontity/now" } ] } The frontend is on Vercel and backend is on Siteground. For example at this link here https://davidc213.sg-host.com/wp-json/acf/v3/options/theme-wwt-settings the field `* case_studies_copy: "

We plan, make and grow experiences that matter.

",` has a full stop at the end. That is not reflecting on the front end, link here https://wwt-evildonkey.vercel.app/case_studies/ I have checked in incognito mode and in another browser. I can see the updates in the REST API but I don't see the CMS updates. I have built and deployed again after the updates but the changes don't want to show up. There must be something caching somewhere. Is there anything I should do in Frontity or Vercel for the front end to update? Thanks

item.title: WordPress content updates not reflecting on the front end - Siteground back end
item.dc:creator: jeffceriello
item.description:
I have figured out that the caching was happening on the Siteground side. Installing the SG Cachepress plugin fixes this problem.

Read full topic
item.source: WordPress content updates not reflecting on the front end - Siteground back end

item.title: WordPress content updates not reflecting on the front end - Siteground back end
item.dc:creator: jeffceriello
item.description:
Hi guys,

Is Frontity or Vercel caching the WordPress content somehow? I have made changes in the CMS but they are not reflecting on the front end.

I have created the file vercel.json

{
    "version": 2,
    "builds": [
        {
            "src": "package.json",
            "use": "@frontity/now"
        }
    ]
}

The frontend is on Vercel and backend is on Siteground.

For example at this link here https://davidc213.sg-host.com/wp-json/acf/v3/options/theme-wwt-settings the field

* case_studies_copy: "<h1 class="p1">We plan, make and grow experiences that matter.</h1> ",

has a full stop at the end. That is not reflecting on the front end, link here https://wwt-evildonkey.vercel.app/case_studies/

I have checked in incognito mode and in another browser. I can see the updates in the REST API but I don’t see the CMS updates.

I have built and deployed again after the updates but the changes don’t want to show up. There must be something caching somewhere.

Is there anything I should do in Frontity or Vercel for the front end to update?

Thanks

Read full topic
item.source: WordPress content updates not reflecting on the front end - Siteground back end
